description_long: This object consists of a clustered group of six pumpkins, with three being a classic orange color and three a pale green. Each pumpkin has a distinct ribbed surface and a short, brownish stem protruding from the top. The pumpkins are arranged closely together, with some partially overlapping, creating a natural, organic pile. The surfaces are matte with a slightly rough texture, typical of real pumpkins, and the stems are dry and woody in appearance. There are no visible carvings, facial features, or decorative accessories on any of the pumpkins. The setting is neutral, with no clear indication of a specific placement location.
description: A group of six pumpkins, three orange and three pale green, with ribbed surfaces and short stems, arranged in a natural pile.
description_view_0: Three orange pumpkins are in the foreground, with three pale green pumpkins behind them, all showing ribbed surfaces and visible stems.
description_view_1: The orange pumpkins dominate the front, with the green pumpkins mostly visible in the background, highlighting the color contrast.
description_view_2: The pale green pumpkins are now in the foreground, with the orange pumpkins behind, showing the grouping from a different angle.
description_view_3: The green pumpkins are most prominent, with the orange pumpkins partially visible behind, emphasizing the roundness and ribbing of the green ones.
